business.headline: Will India raise ethanol blending beyond 20%? Here's what the Minister saysResponding to concerns about vehicle performance, the Minister said the government has not received any widespread or verified complaints from vehicle manufacturers, automobile associations or consumer groups regarding engine failure, fuel pump problems, corrosion or water contamination linked to E20 fuel.

Dalal Street ends lower on weak bank earnings, rising Middle East tensionsThe BSE Sensex fell 442.93 points, or 0.57%, to close at 77,708.52. The NSE Nifty50 also ended in the red, slipping 95.80 points, or 0.39%, to settle at 24,238.50.

Government says no proposal to scrap LTCG tax on equitiesThe government has ruled out scrapping the long-term capital gains tax on listed equities, Finance MoS Pankaj Chaudhary told the Lok Sabha. The government also said LTCG tax collections rose 78% year-on-year, highlighting its growing contribution to revenue.

Rising crude prices hit fuel margins. Why are OMC profits still holding up?Despite weaker fuel marketing margins, integrated profits, which combine refining and fuel marketing performance, remained well above recent averages.

SBI Funds Management IPO lists tomorrow with 18% GMP: Is it a long-term buy?SBI Funds Management is all set to debut on the stock exchanges tomorrow, backed by a strong 18% grey market premium. But does that make it a good long-term investment? Let's have a look.

EPFO pension stopped? Submit Digital Life Certificate from home in 6 easy stepsHas your EPFO pension stopped unexpectedly? One reason could be that your Digital Life Certificate (Jeevan Pramaan) has not been submitted. Here's how to complete the process from home in six easy steps.
Why has Apple increased music subscription prices in India?Apple Music users in India will now have to pay more for their subscriptions. Why has the company decided to increase prices now?

HDFC Bank, Axis Bank fall up to 6%: Why are bank stocks falling today?At around 12.55 pm, HDFC Bank was down 5.1% at Rs 778, while Axis Bank had plunged 5.7% to Rs 1,252.80. Kotak Mahindra Bank also fell 2.6% to Rs 379.85, pulling the Nifty Bank down 883.40 points, or 1.51%, to 57,638.

Accenture has a new way of giving raises. Here's how it worksUnder the new approach, 50% of an approved salary increase will be added to an employee's base salary. The remaining 50% will be paid separately as a one-time lump-sum amount.

Sensex tumbles over 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?At around 12.48 pm, the BSE Sensex was trading at 77,399.31, down 752.14 points (0.96%), while the Nifty50 fell 193.50 points (0.8%) to 24,140.80.

Why the government is cracking down on restaurant service charges nowThe government has stepped up action against restaurants that add service charges by default. The move signals stricter enforcement after court backing and consumer complaints.

Axis Bank appoints ex-Bandhan Bank finance chief Rajeev Mantri as CFOMantri will assume charge on September 28, 2026, subject to the necessary regulatory and statutory approvals, the bank said in a regulatory filing.

JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

India may get Rs 10 and 20 plastic currency notes from next year. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India has begun preparations for a pilot of polymer banknotes by inviting global bids for substrate supply. The trial, likely to start with Rs 10 and Rs 20 notes, will shape any wider rollout.

SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh for misleading consent tactics, loyalty programme opt-insA consumer watchdog finds SpiceJet's booking interface nudged users into loyalty enrolment and promotional consent, raising fresh questions over how digital platforms secure customer approval.
Reliance Q1 profit falls 22% despite 25% jump in revenue. Here's whyReliance Industries posted a 22% drop in first-quarter net profit even as revenue rose sharply.

Founder's life is hard: $1.2 billion fintech CEO says bank rejected home loanDespite a long relationship with the lender, a strong credit score and significant assets, a fintech founder said a leading private sector bank rejected his home loan application.

Apple beats Nvidia to become world's most valuable companyApple's market capitalisation stood at around $4.88 trillion on Friday as its shares remained largely steady. Nvidia, meanwhile, slipped to around $4.86 trillion after its shares fell 3.5%.

Awareness, better policies can unlock India's tourism potential, say expertsSpeaking at the India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, tourism industry representatives said India should focus on promoting year-round travel, creating greater awareness about unique destinations and ensuring stable policies to support the sector.

When no asset looks an easy bet, do multi-asset allocation funds make sense?Equity markets have faced a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions, while gold and silver, traditionally considered safe havens during periods of uncertainty, have also fallen sharply from the all-time highs they touched in January.

entertainment.headline: Swara Bhasker reacts as Mumbai police arrests husband Fahad from CJP protest site Swara Bhasker shared a video showing Mumbai Police detaining Fahad Ahmad at the Chaityabhoomi protest in Mumbai. The clip and her post drew attention to the detentions, reported injuries and the wider CJP mobilisation in Delhi.

This is not Thalapathy's last film, it's CM Vijay's first: H Vinoth on Jana Nayagan Ahead of Jana Nayagan's release, director H Vinoth said the film should be viewed as Chief Minister Vijay's first rather than the actor's farewell film.

Avengers: Doomsday trailer unveils Doctor Doom, Chris Evans' surprise return. Watch Marvel Studios has released the first full trailer for Avengers: Doomsday, with Robert Downey Jr unveiled as Doctor Doom. The footage also teases Chris Evans' surprise appearance and sets up a major Avengers showdown.

Shabana Azmi shares video from CJP protest, says salute to all fellow protestors Shabana Azmi joined thousands of protesters marching towards Parliament after backing the CJP agitation at Jantar Mantar. She said the demonstrators were exercising their constitutional right to a peaceful protest as the Monsoon Session began.

Jana Nayagan new promo shows CM Vijay's action-packed avatar. Watch After months of uncertainty caused by a censor row and an HD leak, Jana Nayagan has finally unveiled fresh footage. The new promo gives fans their first major look at Vijay's film in months.

Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi to release in 2026, new poster dismisses delay reports A new poster for Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi has indicated the film will arrive in 2026 despite delay reports. The film directed by Apoorva Lakhia, also stars Chitrangda Singh in a key role.

Prakash Raj waves Constitution, rallies crowd at CJP's Sansad Chalo march. Video Prakash Raj joined the CJP's Chalo Sansad march in Delhi and waved the Constitution after addressing protesters over alleged NEET irregularities. The protest drew support from actors and students as police action and detention allegations intensified the agitation.

After The Odyssey, Christopher Nolan says next film is at least 3 years away Director Christopher Nolan has said he will wait at least three years before directing again after The Odyssey. He said the all-IMAX production pushed his team to the limit and showed why Hollywood must still take risks.

What will they gain?: Actor and BJP MP Hema Malini reacts to CJP protests in Delhi The first day of Parliament's Monsoon Session witnessed an intense protest by the Cockroach Janta Party in central Delhi, leading to clashes with security forces. BJP MP Hema Malini urged for dialogue over disruption and assured that the government will take appropriate action on the issues raised.

Arijit Singh to return from retirement for Emraan Hashmi's Awarapan 2? Singer Arijit Singh has reportedly sung Yeh Awarapan for Awarapan 2, starring Emraan Hashmi. The track could mark his first playback release since announcing he would stop taking new assignments.

Kajal Aggarwal's stand on bold scenes goes viral amid Toxic's intimacy debate Actor Kajal Aggarwal backed personal choice on intimate scenes as Kiara Advani faces criticism over Toxic's song Tabaahi. She also revealed why she has always refused such roles and how motherhood has shaped her decisions.

You cannot arm-twist government: Kangana Ranaut reacts to CJP protest BJP MP Kangana Ranaut has stated that the government cannot be arm-twisted by the Opposition regarding who should remain in office, emphasising that Parliament is the proper forum for debates and accountability. She criticised the ongoing protests disrupting the Monsoon Session, urging for orderly discussions on public issues like the NEET controversy and Ram Mandir donation allegations.

Former Bigg Boss Kannada contestant files police complaint over online harassment Kannada actor Aishwarya Sindogi has approached the police alleging sustained online harassment and abusive comments on social media. An FIR has been filed, and the police have launched an investigation based on the allegations.

Jana Nayagan director explains why one should watch film in theatres despite leak Director H Vinoth has urged audiences to watch Vijay's Jana Nayagan in theatres despite its HD leak. He said the Vijay film tackles sensitive political issues and arrives after the actor's rise to Chief Minister.

Actor Anupama Parameswaran's cryptic posts spark break-up buzz with Dhruv Vikram Actor Anupama Parameswaran returned to Instagram with emotional posts that quickly reignited talk around her actor Dhruv Vikram. Her lines about peace, permissions and choosing herself have fuelled break-up speculation, though neither actor has confirmed anything.

Wife Gautami backs Ram Kapoor after Shreya says he crossed boundaries by kissing her Actor Gautami Kapoor has defended Ram Kapoor after Shreya Kalra accused him of crossing boundaries on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. Her response came as online criticism grew over the reality show exchange and its impact on their family.

Who plays whom in Ramayana? Every actor and character in Nitesh Tiwari's epic Filmmaker Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana has unveiled its ensemble cast, mapping actors to the epic's key characters. The line-up, led by Ranbir Kapoor, Sai Pallavi and Yash, has intensified anticipation for the two-part film.

Is Vijay's Jana Nayagan a remake of Bhagavanth Kesari? Same same, but different Vijay's Jana Nayagan's trailer has renewed comparisons with Balayya's Bhagavanth Kesari over its guardian-and-protege storyline. The similarities stand out further because Vijay's farewell film also carries overt political messaging.

After Ramayana, Yash's Toxic gears up for grand trailer launch in Bengaluru Yash's Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown-Ups is reportedly set to kick off its promotions, with the makers planning a grand trailer launch event in Bengaluru on August 8. This comes weeks after the trailer launch of Ramayana.

general news.headline: 4 Indians killed in attack on cargo ship leaving Ukraine; govt condemns strikeThe Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) said the Guinea-Bissau-flagged vessel MV Golden Leo came under attack on the evening of July 19 as it sailed out of Odesa with 17 crew members on board, including five Indians. Ukraine blames Russian cruise missiles for attack.

5 IPS officers among 50 cops hurt, shops vandalised as CJP protest turns violent  Live updatesTwo police buses were attacked, shops on Janpath were allegedly vandalised and more than 50 security personnel were injured. Now, multiple FIRs and a possible conspiracy probe are widening the investigation.

Free Wangchuk, sack Pradhan, Rs 1 crore for each NEET death: CJP's 3 demandsA CJP delegation met Health Minister JP Nadda and submitted a memorandum listing three key demands – the release of activist Sonam Wangchuk from the hospital, the removal of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, and Rs 1 crore compensation for families of NEET aspirants who allegedly died by suicide following the NEET paper leak.

Accenture has a new way of giving raises. Here's how it worksUnder the new approach, 50% of an approved salary increase will be added to an employee's base salary. The remaining 50% will be paid separately as a one-time lump-sum amount.
Abhijeet Dipke is the leader of the vacuumThe Cockroach Janta Party is not about Abhijeet Dipke. It is about the vacancy he filled.

Tripura top cop Anurag Dhankar found hanging in office, suicide suspectedTripura DGP Anurag Dhankar was declared dead at 12:48 pm on Monday despite nearly 48 minutes of CPR after being brought to hospital without vital signs. Authorities have launched an investigation, while the exact cause of death will be determined after a post-mortem examination.

How Abhijeet Dipke pulls a Houdini, each time, every timeActivist Sonam Wangchuk's fast gave the CJP protest its defining moments. CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke, meanwhile, seemed to reappear after each defining moment. Whether it was Wangchuk's detention, the march to the Parliament on Monday or the confusion over his own alleged detention.

Government says no proposal to scrap LTCG tax on equitiesThe government has ruled out scrapping the long-term capital gains tax on listed equities, Finance MoS Pankaj Chaudhary told the Lok Sabha. The government also said LTCG tax collections rose 78% year-on-year, highlighting its growing contribution to revenue.

Water's greatest mystery solved? India-born physicist's science-defying discoveryHandcrafted ByOur Editors For over a century, electric sparks fired into water have produced explosions stronger than science can explain. An 84-year-old Indian-origin physicist claims he has finally solved the mystery, and says the answer points to clean energy from ordinary water.

Hunger strike will continue till youth leaders meet MPs: Sonam WangchukWangchuk issued a handwritten statement through his wife, Gitanjali Angmo, saying he would continue his fast, now in its 23rd day. He criticised the police action against peaceful protesters and urged the government to allow student representatives to present their grievances before Parliament.

Live: Delhi Police probe Pak-linked Social Media handles over violence claimsCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Senior leaders of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) met Union Minister J.P. Nadda on Monday and submitted a charter of demands, even as thousands of protesters took part in the outfit's 'Chalo Sansad' march amid unprecedented security in the national capital. The march began from Jantar Mantar in the morning, with protesters attempting to make their way towards Parliament. Clashes broke out at several locations as police stopped the march, prompting security personnel to resort to lathicharge and tear gas to disperse the crowd. Authorities have enforced prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) across the New Delhi district as part of elaborate security arrangements. The CJP has organised the march to protest alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, demand sweeping education reforms and seek the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates on the CJP's 'Chalo Sansad' march.

He is very caring and kind: Siya's engagement video with Ketan resurfacesAn old engagement video shows Siya Goyal calling Ketan Agarwal very caring and kind. The clip has resurfaced amid the alleged murder case, drawing intense reactions online.

Ramayana trailer belongs to Yash: How Ravana emerges as its biggest forceYash's Ravana was unveiled as Ramayana's most commanding force during a special trailer screening in Delhi. Here's why the actor's presence could shape the film's early buzz before the trailer debuts online on July 24.

Prakash Raj waves Constitution, rallies crowd at CJP's Sansad Chalo march. VideoPrakash Raj joined the CJP's Chalo Sansad march in Delhi and waved the Constitution after addressing protesters over alleged NEET irregularities. The protest drew support from actors and students as police action and detention allegations intensified the agitation.

India's top economists say skip college, but India still wants 50% in itHandcrafted ByOur Editors Several of India's top government advisers now tell students to skip college, even as youth unemployment hits 16 percent and IT layoffs mount. But the "skip college" push has a hole: Musk's Tesla still hires mostly degree-holders, and India's own policy targets 50 percent college enrolment by 2035 — the opposite ask.

Will India raise ethanol blending beyond 20%? Here's what the Minister saysResponding to concerns about vehicle performance, the Minister said the government has not received any widespread or verified complaints from vehicle manufacturers, automobile associations or consumer groups regarding engine failure, fuel pump problems, corrosion or water contamination linked to E20 fuel.

1964: How NASA helped build India's first Rocketry InstituteIt was 1964. ISRO had not yet been established, but in Ranchi, India's first rocketry department was already taking shape. Built with support from NASA and backed by Homi J Bhabha and Vikram Sarabhai, it laid the foundation for India's early space research. Today, the department continues to produce space engineers, with four BIT Mesra alumni playing a key role in Skyroot Aerospace's successful Vikram-I mission.

Video: Drought-hit Andhra villagers hold frog wedding to pray for rainVillagers caught two frogs from a nearby pond to serve as bride and groom, adorned them with turmeric and saffron and solemnised their wedding in the presence of village elders.

Trouble for Pak on four fronts. Is a new nation on the horizon?From Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa to POK and the Afghan border, Pakistan is facing four distinct but increasingly interconnected crises. Together, they reveal the erosion of state authority and the limits of Asim Munir's security-first approach. A former Afghan leader suggests that a new country might be taking shape.

Punjab farmers head to Delhi for July 21 Mahapanchayat against US trade dealPunjab farmers are heading to Delhi for a July 21 Mahapanchayat against the proposed India-US trade agreement. Farm leaders say the pact could hurt small cultivators, traders and jobs, and have warned of a bigger agitation.

Is anti-anxiety jewellery becoming Gen Z's discreet stress buster?Fidget spinners and stress balls have long been go-to tools for restless hands. Now, there's another addition to the list: jewellery that claims to soothe anxiety while looking effortlessly chic. But can it actually work?

15-hour workdays, 4 months without pay: Hyderabad woman seeks rescue from OmanShabnam Begum from Hyderabad has sought help from the Indian Embassy in Muscat after alleging abuse by her employer in Oman. Her family has sought her safe return and action against the agent who allegedly sent her on false promises.

UK skilled workers visa used by kebab shops, mosques, gurdwaras. But how?Introduced after Brexit to attract highly skilled professionals, the UK's Skilled Worker visa is facing growing scrutiny. British media reports and politicians claim sponsor licences issued to corner shops, restaurants, kebab shops and even religious organisations are being used to recruit workers for roles that they say fall outside the scheme's original purpose.

Nepal approves redesigned Re 1 coin, drops disputed map linked to India border rowNepal's Council of Ministers on Thursday approved a proposal from the Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) to mint redesigned one-rupee and two-rupee coins.

sports.headline: UEFA president boycotts World Cup final in protest against FIFA over Balogun saga UEFA president Aleksander Ceferin stayed away from the FIFA World Cup final as tensions with world football's governing body escalated over the controversial suspension of Folarin Balogun's one-match ban.

India spends over Rs 53 crore on preparations ahead of Glasgow Games The Sports Ministry has spent over Rs 53 crore on preparing Indian athletes for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games, with athletics, boxing and weightlifting receiving the biggest share of the funding.

Rudi Garcia steps down as Belgium coach after World Cup quarterfinal exit Belgium parted ways with head coach Rudi Garcia, the Royal Belgian Football Association (RBFA) announced on Monday. The Frenchman guided the Red Devils to the quarter-finals of the World Cup, where they lost to eventual champions Spain.

PV Sindhu leads India's challenge at China Open after historic Japan Open triumph Fresh off her historic Japan Open triumph, PV Sindhu will lead India's campaign at the China Open Super 1000 tournament as she looks to carry her winning momentum into Changzhou.

Sindhu's Japan Open triumph no surprise for Saina: Never write a champion off Saina Nehwal said PV Sindhu's Japan Open title shows she remains a serious contender on the tour. Sindhu, 31, won her first title since Decmeber 2024, beating Akane Yamaguchi in the final on Sunday in Tokyo.

Paula Badosa suffers major heartbreak, forced to retire from Iasi Open final Paula Badosa's resurgence suffered a heartbreaking setback after the Spaniard retired from the Iasi Open final against Mayar Sherif while trailing 6-4, 4-0, ending her nine-match winning streak.

Rohit Sharma's Lord's hundred put an end to retirement speculation: Saba Karim Saba Karim said Rohit Sharma's Lord's century ended the retirement speculation surrounding the veteran opener, adding that India's priority now is settling their pace attack ahead of the New Zealand tour.

Shubman Gill frustrated with India's injury setbacks, sends strong message. Shubman Gill has raised concerns over India's growing injury problems, questioning the team's fitness standards ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup. The India captain said repeated setbacks forced the team management to constantly alter combinations during the England series.

Tennis body mandates one-time eligibility test for female players The WTA has introduced a new rule requiring female players to undergo a one-time SRY gene test to confirm their eligibility to compete on the women's tour. The policy, which comes into effect on Tuesday, replaces the organisation's previous transgender participation regulations.

Argentina's obsession with dark arts finally backfires in World Cup final Argentina's trademark intensity delivered trophies for five years, but it deserted them when it mattered most. As Spain lifted the World Cup, Lionel Messi was left watching a final that never gave him the chance to shape the game on football's biggest stage.

Back Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli or move on: Yuvraj Singh's strong message to BCCI Yuvraj Singh has urged the BCCI and team management to give Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli clarity over their ODI futures, saying the selectors must clearly communicate whether the veteran duo remain part of India's plans for the 2027 World Cup.

Great to cheap? Messi under fire for shocking attempt to get Cucurella sent offLionel Messi faces fierce criticism for a cynical attempt to get Spain's Marc Cucurella sent off during the World Cup final on Sunday. Argentina's defeat ended in an ugly post-match brawl.

Watch: Carlos Alcaraz, Rafael Nadal erupt in joy after Spain's World Cup gloryCarlos Alcaraz and Rafael Nadal celebrated on social media after Spain beat Argentina in the FIFA World Cup final. Ferran Torres's extra-time winner capped Spain's title run and triggered jubilation across the country.

FIFA World Cup Final: Luis de la Fuente takes Spain back to promised landLuis de la Fuente completed one of international football's greatest managerial runs as Spain beat Argentina to win the FIFA World Cup 2026. The veteran coach hailed the unity and values of the Spanish squad that transformed a talented generation into world champions.

No noise, no fun: Rohit Sharma puts retirement rumours to bed in explosive interviewEngland vs India: Speaking to the BCCI after his century at Lord's on Sunday, Rohit Sharma dismissed the outside noise surrounding his future. Ahead of the series finale in London, speculation had been rife that the selectors were ready to move on from the 39-year-old and that he would announce his retirement after the match.

I am not that ugly: Spain boss De la Fuente says it is tattoo time for CucurellaMarc Cucurella promised to tattoo Luis de la Fuente's face if Spain won the World Cup, and now the coach expects him to follow through. Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the World Cup final on Sunday, July 19

Messy, not Messi: 0 shots, 0 spark as Argentina great goes missing in finalFIFA World Cup 2026: Spain did not stop Lionel Messi with brute force or relentless man-marking. They simply denied him the spaces where he has spent two decades deciding football matches.

Lamine hug, paparazzi poses: How Keyne Yamal celebrated Spain's World Cup winKeyne Yamal marked Spain's World Cup triumph with a hug for Lamine, camera poses and kickabouts on the pitch. His playful presence has turned him into an unlikely mascot for Spain and a viral sensation throughout the tournament.

Scaloni unlikely to continue as Argentina coach after World Cup heartbreakLionel Scaloni said he is likely to step aside as Argentina coach at the end of his current contract following the World Cup final defeat to Spain. Scaloni left the decision in the hands of the Argentina football federation.

Shoaib Akhtar enters Messi-Ronaldo GOAT debate. Cricket fans ask whyShoaib Akhtar used a World Cup final panel to argue that Cristiano Ronaldo is football's biggest brand but Lionel Messi the better player. But the cricket fans asked a simple question. Why was the Pakistan legend present on the panel of a football show?

Argentina coach in tears, leaves press meet midway after World Cup final defeatArgentina coach Lionel Scaloni broke down during his post-match press conference after Spain ended his side's World Cup title defence. Scaloni praised Lionel Messi and his players while admitting the defeat "hurts our souls" after the 1-0 final loss.

World Cup was written in the stars for Spain: Ferran Torres after beating ArgentinaFerran Torres said Spain's World Cup triumph over Argentina was "written in the stars" after scoring the extra-time winner in the final. The forward admitted Lionel Messi always brings nerves but hailed Spain's commitment to their style of football.

FIFA World Cup awards: Did Rodri deserve Golden Ball over Messi?FIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri won the Golden Ball after captaining Spain to a 1-0 extra-time win over Argentina in the World Cup final. The award stirred immediate debate as many felt Lionel Messi's eight-goal campaign deserved the top individual honour.

World Cup final ends in ugly brawl: Argentina star goes rogue, shown redLeandro Paredes clashed with two Spanish footballers after Argentina's embarrassing loss in the FIFA World Cup final. This happened moments after the final whistle was blown and Spain were crowned as the world champions.

Trump refuses to leave podium in awkward moment as Spain lift World Cup: VideoUS President Donald Trump was briefly asked to move aside during Spain's World Cup trophy celebrations after presenting the trophy to captain Rodri. The moment quickly went viral as officials cleared the stage for Spain's players to lift football's

Lionel Messi moved to tears after FIFA World Cup Final heartbreakLionel Messi was overcome with emotion after Argentina's FIFA World Cup title defence ended in heartbreak. Messi broke down in tears as Spain defeated the defending champions 1-0 in the Extra Time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title.

Lionel Messi hugs Lamine Yamal, passes the torch after Spain's World Cup winLionel Messi and Lamine Yamal shared an emotional embrace after Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time to win the FIFA World Cup.

Spain make Messi's Argentina look toothless to win 2nd World CupFIFA World Cup Final: Spain wore down Argentina before Ferran Torres scored the extra-time winner at MetLife Stadium. The goal ended Lionel Messi's title defence and delivered Spain a second World Cup crown.

Spain fans rage after VAR chalks off Nico Williams' World Cup final goalWorld Cup final: Spain were left raging after Nico Williams' goal in the World Cup final against Argentina was ruled out by VAR for a foul in the build-up. The controversial decision sparked a social media storm, with many fans insisting the contact was too minimal to cancel the goal.

World Cup final: Lisandro Martinez's fiery display ends in injury, sparks frenzy among Man United fansArgentina defender Lisandro Martinez's combative World Cup final came to a painful end after he was forced off injured moments after receiving the first yellow card of the match. The setback sparked concern among Manchester United fans ahead of the new season.

Shubman Gill breaks silence on Rohit retirement rumours after Lord's hundredIndia vs England: India captain Shubman Gill dismissed speculation over Rohit Sharma's ODI future after the defeat to England at Lord's, saying there had been no discussion within the team about the veteran opener's retirement despite reports linking the 39-year-old with an international exit after the England series.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
